title: SMART goals
description: Practice understanding and application of SMART goals

assessment_items.question_content:
SMART is a way of making goals that help you achieve what you want. SMART stands for five words that start with the letters S, M, A, R, and T. Each word tells you something important about your goal. 

**What does the *A* in SMART stand for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Aggressive
Achievable
Adjustable
Accountable

assessment_items.question_content:
SMART is a way of making goals that help you achieve what you want. SMART stands for five words that start with the letters S, M, A, R, and T. Each word tells you something important about your goal. 

**What does the *S* in SMART stand for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Specific
Simple
Stretch
Solution

assessment_items.question_content:
SMART is a way of making goals that help you achieve what you want. SMART stands for five words that start with the letters S, M, A, R, and T. Each word tells you something important about your goal. 

**What does the *M* in SMART stand for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Measurable
Motivational
Manageable
Meaningful

assessment_items.question_content:
For a goal to be a SMART goal, it needs to be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, and Time-bound.

**Which of these is an example of a *measurable* goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: I want to save more money.
I want to save $\$500$ by the end of the year.
I want to save enough money for a vacation.
I want to save money for a rainy day.

assessment_items.question_content:
For a goal to be a SMART goal, it needs to be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, and Time-bound.

**Which of these is an example of a *specific* goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: I want to be rich.
I want to pay off my credit card debt in six months.
I want to save more money.
I want to buy a house because I don't want to pay rent anymore.

assessment_items.question_content:
For a goal to be a SMART goal, it needs to be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, and Time-bound.

**Which of these is an example of a *realistic* goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: I want to buy a private island.
I want to invest in the stock market and make huge profits.
I want to create a budget and stick to it every month.
I want to win the jackpot in a casino.

assessment_items.question_content:
When we make goals, we can use the word SMART to check if they are good goals or if they need to be improved.

**Which of these is a SMART financial goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: I want to save money.
I want to save $\$500$ for an emergency fund by the end of the year.
I want to save enough money to buy a car someday.
I want to save $10\%$ of my income every month.

assessment_items.question_content:
When we make goals, we can use the word SMART to check if they are good goals or if they need to be improved.

**Which of these is a SMART financial goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: I want to pay off my student loans, my car loan, and my credit card debt.
I want to increase my income.
I want to create a budget and stick to it for three months.
I want to spend less on entertainment.

assessment_items.question_content:
When we make goals, we can use the word SMART to check if they are good goals or if they need to be improved.

**Which of these is a SMART financial goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: I want to invest for retirement.
I want to save up for a down payment of $20\%$ of the purchase price of a car within my budget range by next year.
I want to raise my credit score by $100$ points and get a lower interest rate.
I want to spend less on eating out.

assessment_items.question_content:
Written below is an example of a financial goal that could be made into a SMART goal.

I want to pay off my credit card debt [[☃ dropdown 1]].

**Complete this goal to make it more *specific*.**
hints:
For a goal to be a SMART goal, it needs to be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, and Time-bound. 

A *specific* goal tells you exactly what you want to accomplish, how you will do it, and why it is important.
To make the goal above more *specific*, choose an ending that describes *how* this goal can be met, and include details, such as a *measurable* amount and a *timeline* for reaching this goal.
Choose an ending that includes a *measurable* amount shown in \$'s, and a *timeline* (in months).
I want to pay off my credit card debt **by paying 200 dollars each month.**

choices.content: as soon as possible
with the highest interest rate first
by paying 200 dollars each month
by cutting back on expenses

assessment_items.question_content:
Written below is an example of a financial goal that could be made into a SMART goal.

I want to save up for a vacation  [[☃ dropdown 1]].

**Complete this goal to make it more *specific*.**
hints:
For a goal to be a SMART goal, it needs to be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, and Time-bound. 

A *specific* goal tells you exactly what you want to accomplish, how you will do it, and why it is important.
To make the goal above more *specific*, choose an ending that describes *how* this goal can be met, and include details, such as a *measurable* amount and a *timeline* for reaching this goal.
Choose an ending that includes a *measurable* amount shown in dollars, and a *timeline* (in months).
I want to save up for a vacation **by saving $100$ dollars each month.**

choices.content: next August
trip
by saving 100 dollars each month
with my family

assessment_items.question_content:
Written below is an example of a financial goal that could be made into a SMART goal.

I want to buy a car  [[☃ dropdown 1]].

**Complete this goal to make it more *specific*.**
hints:
For a goal to be a SMART goal, it needs to be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, and Time-bound. 

A *specific* goal tells you exactly what you want to accomplish, how you will do it, and it provides *details* of your goal, such as amount or timeline.
To make the goal above more *specific*, choose an ending that includes details, such as a *measurable* amount and a *timeline* for reaching this goal.
Choose an ending that includes a *measurable* amount shown in numbers, and a *timeline* (in years).
I want to buy a car **that I can pay off in four years or less.**

choices.content: soon
that I can pay off in four years or less
that has all the features I want
that fits my family
